# Generated Files

`ai-sensitive-files generate --out .` writes files derived from
`.ai-sensitive-files/sensitive-files.yaml`.
Do not edit generated files by hand.
Change the policy, then regenerate.

## Common Rules

- Every generated file starts from the same policy.
- Existing `.gitignore` is not edited.
- Existing generated files are only overwritten when their content is the same,
  or when `--force` is used.
- With `--force`, the previous file is saved as `.bak` before overwrite.
- `check` compares generated files with the current policy
  and fails when they are stale.

## Ignore Files

Files:

- `.aiignore`
- `.cursorignore`
- `.copilotignore`
- `.gitignore.ai-sensitive-files`

Format:

```gitignore
# Generated by ai-sensitive-files. Edit .ai-sensitive-files/sensitive-files.yaml, then regenerate.
# ai-sensitive-files: AI ignore entries

.agent-privacy-guard/entities.local.yaml
.aws/credentials
.env
secrets/**
```

Each non-comment line is a repository-relative path or basic wildcard pattern.
The order is sorted for stable diffs.

Source:

- `.aiignore`, `.cursorignore`, and `.copilotignore`
  include entries with `action.aiignore: true`
- `.gitignore.ai-sensitive-files`
  includes entries with `action.gitignore: true`

Usage:

- Treat `.aiignore`, `.cursorignore`, and `.copilotignore`
  as AI/editor ignore intent.
  Agent support differs by tool, so pair them with commit checks.
- Review `.gitignore.ai-sensitive-files`,
  then manually merge chosen entries into `.gitignore`.
  The tool does not edit `.gitignore`.

## Claude Code Deny Read

File:

- `generated/claude-code-deny-read.json`

Format:

```json
{
  "denyRead": [
    ".agent-privacy-guard/entities.local.yaml",
    ".aws/credentials",
    ".env",
    "secrets/**"
  ]
}
```

Source:

- Entries with `action.aiignore: true`

Usage:

- Copy or adapt the `denyRead` array into the Claude Code settings layer
  used by the target repository.
- This file is a snippet, not a complete managed settings file.

## AI Agent Guidance

File:

- `generated/ai-agent-guidance.md`

Format:

```markdown
# AI Agent Guidance

Generated from `.ai-sensitive-files/sensitive-files.yaml`.
Do not edit this file by hand.

AI coding agents and editor assistants should not read,
summarize, index, or paste the following paths into prompts:

- `.env`
- `.agent-privacy-guard/entities.local.yaml`
```

Source:

- Entries with `action.aiignore: true`

Usage:

- Use this as a short instruction snippet for Codex, Cursor,
  Copilot, or local agent instructions.
- It is guidance only. It does not replace tool settings or hooks.

## Summary

File:

- `generated/ai-sensitive-files.summary.md`

Format:

```markdown
# AI Sensitive Files Summary

Generated from policy. Do not edit this file by hand.

- `.env`: local environment secrets (aiignore, gitignore, encrypt, commit_block); decrypted path: `.env`; encrypted path: `.env.sops.yaml`; crypto: `sops-age`, manual edit: `decrypted`
```

Source:

- All `sensitive_files` entries

Usage:

- Review policy decisions without opening the full YAML.
- Useful in pull requests that change sensitive file boundaries.

## Encryption Targets

File:

- `generated/encryption-targets.txt`

Format:

```text
# Generated by ai-sensitive-files. Edit .ai-sensitive-files/sensitive-files.yaml, then regenerate.
# ai-sensitive-files: SOPS/age encryption targets

.agent-privacy-guard/entities.local.sops.yaml
.env.sops.yaml
secrets/**/*.sops.yaml
```

Source:

- Entries with `action.encrypt: true` and `encrypted_path`

Usage:

- Review which repo-local encrypted artifacts are expected.
- Feed into wrapper scripts only after reviewing wildcard behavior.

## Decryption Targets

File:

- `generated/decryption-targets.txt`

Format:

```text
# Generated by ai-sensitive-files. Edit .ai-sensitive-files/sensitive-files.yaml, then regenerate.
# ai-sensitive-files: decrypted plaintext output targets

.env.sops.yaml -> .env
1password:op://Engineering/App CI/.env -> .env.ci
bitwarden:bw://item/app-shared-env/notes -> .env.shared
```

Source:

- Entries with `action.encrypt: true`

Usage:

- Review where plaintext appears during local development.
- Confirm whether the source is a repo-local encrypted file
  or an external secret manager item.

## Secret Sources

File:

- `generated/secret-sources.txt`

Format:

```text
# Generated by ai-sensitive-files. Edit .ai-sensitive-files/sensitive-files.yaml, then regenerate.
# ai-sensitive-files: external secret manager references

1password: op://Engineering/App CI/.env -> .env.ci
bitwarden: bw://item/app-shared-env/notes -> .env.shared
```

Source:

- Entries with `action.encrypt: true` and `crypto.secret_ref`

Usage:

- Review which external secret-manager entries materialize local plaintext.
- This file does not fetch, store, or validate secret-manager credentials.

## Crypto Plan

File:

- `generated/crypto-plan.md`

Format:

```markdown
# AI Sensitive Files Crypto Plan

Generated from policy. Review commands before running them.

## .env

- method: `sops-age`
- encrypted_path: `.env.sops.yaml`
- recipients:
  - `age1exampleteampublickey...`
- decrypted_path: `.env`
- manual_edit: `decrypted`
- encrypt: `sops --encrypt --output .env.sops.yaml .env`
- decrypt: `sops --decrypt --output .env .env.sops.yaml`
```

Source:

- Entries with `action.encrypt: true`
- `crypto.method`
- `crypto.recipients`
- `crypto.encrypt_command`
- `crypto.decrypt_command`
- `crypto.manual_edit`
- `encrypted_path`, `decrypted_path`, and `crypto.secret_ref`

Usage:

- Review the exact commands before running encryption or decryption.
- Use the plan to discuss manual-edit policy in code review.
- Do not treat it as key management. SOPS, age, 1Password,
  and Bitwarden remain responsible for their own trust and auth.
